diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling HoYoverse Player Support! How can I help you today?
[Caller] Hi, I got affected by the outage this week. I see a 300 Primogem mail in my box but I wasn't sure if that was for me or a mistake.
[Agent] That's a great thing to double-check. Let me pull up your account so we can confirm everything lines up. Could I have your UID?
[Caller] 832118007.
[Agent] And for security, your HoYoverse email?
[Caller] ytanaka at email dot com.
[Agent] Perfect, you're verified, Yuki. Yes, that 300 Primogem mail is definitely for you. It's tied to incident INC202604161, the 18-hour Asia region outage from April 16th to 17th. Your UID starts with 83 so you're inside the affected range. Nothing to worry about, totally legitimate, go ahead and claim it.
[Caller] Oh great, I was worried it was some kind of phishing thing.
[Agent] Totally reasonable caution. One way to always tell it's real, the sender on in-game mail from us will show as Paimon with the official System flag, and any compensation mail from HoYoverse will reference an incident ID like the INC202604161 I just mentioned. If it ever looks different, don't claim it, give us a call.
[Caller] That's really useful, thank you. Actually while I have you, I also had a weird thing where I was locked out for an hour during that same window.
[Agent] Let me check your login logs. Yeah, I see two failed connection attempts during the outage window, both within the INC202604161 timeframe. So that's all part of the same incident and already covered in the compensation. Good news, you're fully squared away.
[Caller] Perfect.
[Agent] You have 30 days to claim the mail before it expires. Anything else I can help you with today?
[Caller] No, that's it, thanks!
[Agent] Take care, Yuki, and enjoy Teyvat!
